author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
Infants are Not Judged According to that Which They are Foreknown as Likely to Do If They Should Live.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: Infants are Not Judged According to that Which They are Foreknown as Likely to Do If They Should Live.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For you see, beloved, how absurd it is, and how foreign from soundness of faith and sincerity of truth, for us to say that infants, when they die, should be judged according to those things which they are foreknown to be going to do if they should live.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For to this opinion, from which certainly every human feeling, on however little reason it may be founded, and especially every Christian feeling, revolts, they are compelled to advance who have chosen in such wise to be withdrawn from the error of the Pelagians as still to think that they must believe, and, moreover, must profess in argument, that the grace of God, through Jesus Christ our Lord, by which alone after the fall of the first man, in whom we all fell, help is afforded to us, is given according to our merits.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And this belief Pelagius himself, before the Eastern bishops as judges, condemned in fear of his own condemnation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And if this be not said of the good or bad works of those who have died, which they would have done if they had lived,—and thus of no works, and works that would never exist, even in the foreknowledge of God,—if this, therefore, be not said, and you see under how great a mistake it is said, what will remain but that we confess, when the darkness of contention is removed, that the grace of God is not given according to our merits, which position the catholic Church defends against the Pelagian heresy; and that we see this in more evident truth especially in infants?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For God is not compelled by fate to come to the help of these infants, and not to come to the help of those,—since the case is alike to both.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Or shall we think that human affairs in the case of infants are not managed by Divine Providence, but by fortuitous chances, when rational souls are either to be condemned or delivered, although, indeed, not a sparrow falls to the ground without the will of our Father which is in heaven?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Or must we so attribute it to the negligence of parents that infants die without baptism, as that heavenly judgments have nothing to do with it; as if they themselves who in this way die badly had of their own will chosen the negligent parents for themselves of whom they were born?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What shall I say when an infant expires some time before he can possibly be advantaged by the ministry of baptism?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For often when the parents are eager and the ministers prepared for giving baptism to the infants, it still is not given, because God does not choose; since He has not kept it in this life for a little while in order that baptism might be given it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What, moreover, when sometimes aid could be afforded by baptism to the children of unbelievers, that they should not go into perdition, and could not be afforded to the children of believers?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: In which case it is certainly shown that there is no acceptance of persons with God; otherwise He would rather deliver the children of His worshippers than the children of His enemies.
